title: malus sativa
intro: Malus sativa, also known as the wild apple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The medicinal properties of Malus sativa are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. Malus sativa can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
